result: {"verdict":"accepted","guard_confirmed":true,"reviewed_sha":"f8f3c517f5f0a12857c4b027f76043dc97bc58e6","base_sha":"2ed3ead9603e7e7dd0a55e995a82c632cd214e77","comments":["crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:285 — no material issue: the acknowledged non-cloneable control, exact-StreamId LIFO ledger, two-phase admission released under the sampler's registry mutex, ordered idempotent Seal, JoinedThenEnded/AlreadyEnded terminal settlement, observable delivery/ack failures with exactly-one rejected-sink finish, and preserved first-error-wins implement the goal correctly and match the Active plan's §3 design; fmt, strict workspace clippy, and the full workspace test suite are green at f8f3c51.","crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:873 — guard proof performed: (a) leaking the retire authority so REMOVE settles without applying to the exact worker turned elastic_busy_retire_acks_exact_lifo_member_at_payload_boundary, elastic_idle_retire_wakes_exact_member_and_unregisters_its_probe, and sealed_running_remove_is_retire_marked_not_already_ended red (timed out awaiting the retired worker's END); (b) selecting logical_lifo.first() instead of .last() turned the exact-identity guards red (RetireMarked StreamId(10) vs expected StreamId(99)); exact head was restored after each mutation, the restored guards pass green, and the worktree is clean at f8f3c517f5f0a12857c4b027f76043dc97bc58e6.","crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:719 — minor, non-blocking: the terminal-ADD private disconnected queue (no stealing of pre-Seal payloads) is not itself pinned by a red-turning test — elastic_add_after_end_of_stream_just_finishes_the_sink joins on an already-drained queue, so replacing the private queue with the shared one stays green; exactly-once still holds via dequeue-ownership, and terminal-race proofs are explicitly ldt-3 scope, but add a guard there.","crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1314 — minor, non-blocking: close_payloads returns via `?` before dropping payload_tx when control is None; unreachable today (only finish() takes the control, after its own close_payloads call), but if a refactor made it reachable, finish() would join a pipeline that never sees end-of-stream — dropping payload_tx before the control check would remove the latent hang shape.","crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1144 — nit: the add_stream doc comment still frames a pipeline-gone ADD as benign cleanup ('closed cleanly so the peer's worker sees its END'); the sink is still finished, but that path now correctly faults the session per plan §3 while the healthy completion race takes the sealed JoinedThenEnded path — reword in a later slice.","crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:493 — style, non-blocking: execute_sink_pipeline_elastic now spans ~780 lines with nested types (MembershipLedger, MemberRecord, PendingReply) and inline failure handling repeated in four arms; extracting the ledger into a named type with mark/reap/fail methods before ldt-2 builds the controller on top would keep the next slice reviewable."]}

comments: crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:285 — no material issue: the acknowledged non-cloneable control, exact-StreamId LIFO ledger, two-phase admission released under the sampler's registry mutex, ordered idempotent Seal, JoinedThenEnded/AlreadyEnded terminal settlement, observable delivery/ack failures with exactly-one rejected-sink finish, and preserved first-error-wins implement the goal correctly and match the Active plan's §3 design; fmt, strict workspace clippy, and the full workspace test suite are green at f8f3c51.

crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:873 — guard proof performed: (a) leaking the retire authority so REMOVE settles without applying to the exact worker turned elastic_busy_retire_acks_exact_lifo_member_at_payload_boundary, elastic_idle_retire_wakes_exact_member_and_unregisters_its_probe, and sealed_running_remove_is_retire_marked_not_already_ended red (timed out awaiting the retired worker's END); (b) selecting logical_lifo.first() instead of .last() turned the exact-identity guards red (RetireMarked StreamId(10) vs expected StreamId(99)); exact head was restored after each mutation, the restored guards pass green, and the worktree is clean at f8f3c517f5f0a12857c4b027f76043dc97bc58e6.

crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:719 — minor, non-blocking: the terminal-ADD private disconnected queue (no stealing of pre-Seal payloads) is not itself pinned by a red-turning test — elastic_add_after_end_of_stream_just_finishes_the_sink joins on an already-drained queue, so replacing the private queue with the shared one stays green; exactly-once still holds via dequeue-ownership, and terminal-race proofs are explicitly ldt-3 scope, but add a guard there.

crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1314 — minor, non-blocking: close_payloads returns via `?` before dropping payload_tx when control is None; unreachable today (only finish() takes the control, after its own close_payloads call), but if a refactor made it reachable, finish() would join a pipeline that never sees end-of-stream — dropping payload_tx before the control check would remove the latent hang shape.

crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/data_plane.rs:1144 — nit: the add_stream doc comment still frames a pipeline-gone ADD as benign cleanup ('closed cleanly so the peer's worker sees its END'); the sink is still finished, but that path now correctly faults the session per plan §3 while the healthy completion race takes the sealed JoinedThenEnded path — reword in a later slice.

crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs:493 — style, non-blocking: execute_sink_pipeline_elastic now spans ~780 lines with nested types (MembershipLedger, MemberRecord, PendingReply) and inline failure handling repeated in four arms; extracting the ledger into a named type with mark/reap/fail methods before ldt-2 builds the controller on top would keep the next slice reviewable.
